body:
## Problem statement

When the Swagger definition does not specify the schemes and no `--default-scheme` is specified on the CLI `go-swagger` will currently choose HTTP. Would you be open to changing this default `--default-scheme` to HTTPS?

The benefit to using HTTPS as the default is it is more secure. The downside is that changing this might break existing users that expect the default `--default-scheme` to be HTTP.

## Background

In https://github.com/grpc-ecosystem/grpc-gateway/issues/1069 we changed `grpc-gateway` so that it only adds a `schemes` key to the Swagger definition if the user explicitly specifies one or more schemes. This was done to support generating a Swagger definition with no schemes (which means to use the scheme used to access the definition itself).

We tested a few generators and in the case of `go-swagger` we found that this would cause clients to start using HTTP instead of HTTPS. Hence this issue.

## Swagger specification

Any Swagger definition without `schemes` will do:

```yaml
swagger: "2.0"
info:
  version: "1.0"
  title: "Hello World"
paths:
  /hello:
    get:
      description: Returns a hello message.
      responses:
        200:
          description: The hello message.
          schema:
            type: string
```

## Steps to reproduce

Generating a client with `./swagger_linux_amd64 generate client -f hello_world.swagger.yaml` will produce a client that uses HTTP:

```
client/hello_world_client.go:var DefaultSchemes = []string{"http"}
```

## Environment

```
]$ ./swagger_linux_amd64 version
version: v0.21.0
commit: 7c0fc3ee340f9d99d85573bc54a57e303a639692
```
title: Use HTTPS as more secure default for --default-scheme

body:
## Problem statement

I have defined several services in my Swagger 2.0 YAML specification. I want to handle every service in slightly different way, but I ran into two problems:

1. I cannot define specific HTTP headers (CORS, caching, `Access-Control` headers, domain-specific headers, etc.) for every service individually, since I have no access to HTTP response in `api.<service>Handler` function. I can add a filter to `setupGlobalMiddleware` and `setupMiddlewares`, but it affects every service.
1. There is no way to return service-specific user-defined error response when request parameters validation fails in the generated code. There is a solution to copy `ServeError` function from https://github.com/go-openapi/errors/blob/v0.19.2/api.go#L119 and write my own function, as proposed here: #1041, #1061, #2060. However, I cannot distinguish the service that has failed.

Both problems would be solvable if I had an access to information about the currently running service. One way is to dispatch services de-facto manually according to the path in HTTP request, the same way as in https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/v0.21.0/generator/templates/server/builder.gotmpl#L317, but it is error-prone and duplicates the generated code.

I have read in several issues that custom templates might be used. Is it the way to go? I looked at the default templates and have some ideas for both problems:

1. Add a handler wrapper over `New<service>(o.context, o.<service>Handler))` expression here: https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/v0.21.0/generator/templates/server/builder.gotmpl#L327.
2. Add a function that transforms response error to a different error here: https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/v0.21.0/generator/templates/server/operation.gotmpl#L70

Could these extensions be defined as generated properties in the API structure? If they were `nil`, the behavior would be the same as now. Do you have better solution?

## Steps to reproduce
`swagger generate server -f <specification>.yaml --strict-additional-properties --keep-spec-order --exclude-spec --exclude-main --model-package=objects --api-package=communication --server-package=config`

## Environment
swagger version: v0.21.0 (2019-10-19)
go version: go1.13.3 linux/amd64
OS: Ubuntu 19.10

body:
## Problem statement

When I have markdown comments in my `swagger:meta` that may container various info fields, they sometimes get parsed and end comment parsing.

## Steps to reproduce

Example Meta schema
```
Package docs API

Documentation for API.

# Authentication
Clients should contact the api first. If the api requires authentication it will return a `401 Unauthorized`
response with a `WWW-Authenticate` header detailing how to authenticate to this api.

The api will first return this response:

``
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
Server: nginx/1.17.5
Date: Fri, 06 Dec 2019 17:40:32 GMT
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Content-Length: 79
Connection: keep-alive
Api-Version: 3.2.0
Www-Authenticate: Bearer realm="https://example.com/oauth/token",scope="example:read"

{"status":"error","error":"access to the requested resource is not authorized"}
``

Note the HTTP Response Header indicating the auth challenge:

``
Www-Authenticate: Bearer realm="https://example.com/oauth/token",scope="example:read"
``

	Schemes: https
	BasePath: /v1
	Version: 1.0.0

	Consumes:
	- application/json

swagger:meta
```

Now currently the `Api-Version` gets parsed as the info `Version` and stops the rest of the comment parsing.

## Solution

Update the regular expressions for meta fields to check for space or start of line first.
Example:
```
^\s*[Vv]ersion\p{Zs}*:\p{Zs}*(.+)$
```
This way the version has to start at beginning of line or with spaces and other characters will invalidate it.

I can update all the meta fields regex and submit a pull request if you think this is a good solution.

body:
## Problem statement

Hello,

small introduction:
As described in #2156, i'm working on generating and testing client library for a swagger specified on:
https://vdc-download.vmware.com/vmwb-repository/dcr-public/6faa975e-68ee-4c6c-944a-5e9e664b2475/50c61a14-cc14-4adb-b936-b5411ccdd1a5/swagger-3.3.2-dist.json.
Which is latest 3.3.2 version from https://code.vmware.com/apis/784/velocloud-sdwan-vco-api

Fixed all items for duplicated definitions, and a few other "cornercases" which are addressed in #2161 for follow-up.

The swagger version derived from that, with all required fixes are at:
[Manual fixed swagger](https://iafilius.home.xs4all.nl/swagger-3.3.2-dist-manualfixes7.json)


Client generated with:
```
../go-swagger/cmd/swagger/swagger generate client -f ./swagger-3.3.2-dist-manualfixes7.json ...
```

It looks like overlap with issue #1707 which is open for more then a year. (but not 100% sure)

While starting with the most simple API call in library (apart from logging in), i need to give the 'EdgeEdgeProvisionBody' struct as API call parameter.

As i'm lookin into go-swagger as an option to generate the client library (opposed to maven/swagger UI), with special focus on a few known issues like:
- not submitting 'non defined and optional(omitempty) types' (structs, bool's, int's, strings etc), even when a possible default value is zero (by design)
- submitting 'defined and optional (omitempty) typed' (structs, bool's, int's, strings etc), even when value is zero.

Using pointer's to (nested) types (struct, string, bool, int ...) is a good working solution path, and also practiced in go-swagger.
But it looks like the produced code contains an error.
For example, the first struct i noticed:
```
/*EdgeEdgeProvisionBody edge_edge_provision
swagger:model EdgeEdgeProvisionBody
*/
type EdgeEdgeProvisionBody struct {

	// challenge password
	ChallengePassword string `json:"challengePassword,omitempty"`

	// configuration Id
	// Required: true
	ConfigurationID *int64 `json:"configurationId"`

	// description
	Description string `json:"description,omitempty"`

	// enterprise Id
	EnterpriseID int64 `json:"enterpriseId,omitempty"`

	// generate certificate
	GenerateCertificate bool `json:"generateCertificate,omitempty"`

	// ha enabled
	HaEnabled bool `json:"haEnabled,omitempty"`

	// model number
	// Required: true
	// Enum: [edge500 edge5X0 edge510 edge6X0 edge840 edge1000 edge1000qat edge3X00 virtual]
	ModelNumber *string `json:"modelNumber"`

	// name
	Name string `json:"name,omitempty"`

	// private key password
	PrivateKeyPassword string `json:"privateKeyPassword,omitempty"`

	// serial number
	SerialNumber string `json:"serialNumber,omitempty"`

	// site
	Site *models.Site `json:"site,omitempty"`

	// subject c n
	SubjectCN string `json:"subjectCN,omitempty"`

	// subject o
	SubjectO string `json:"subjectO,omitempty"`

	// subject o u
	SubjectOU string `json:"subjectOU,omitempty"`
}
```

Solution for nested structs looks OK:
```
	// site
	Site *models.Site `json:"site,omitempty"`
```

Solution for Mandatory values has the pointer solution, so it will submit the parameter but with null value, like:
'''
"configurationId":null,
'''
This is perfectly fine.

But the types which are defined defined as optional (omitempty) are not defined as pointers. A typical sample:
```
	// ha enabled
	HaEnabled bool `json:"haEnabled,omitempty"`
```
So in this case, the boolean only wil be submitted when having the true value, and false value is always ignored. (empty/not defined != false)

The same with an int:
```
	// enterprise Id
	EnterpriseID int64 `json:"enterpriseId,omitempty"`

```
So a valid value of '0' always will be ignored.
This last case (omitempty without pointer solution) should be changed to a using a pointer as well.
Removing the 'omitempty' does not solve it, it introduces the issue that value always is send, even when not defiend (as null, when using pointer).
In this case the API server is quite unpredictable when submitting parameters is is not expecting, introducing lots of undesired effects.

Regards,

Arjan


## Swagger specification
2.0
## Steps to reproduce

See above description, 

## Environment
swagger version: 
../go-swagger/cmd/swagger/swagger version
dev

// Decimal represents a decimal number, which is a precise decimal representation not subject to floating point issues.

## Problem statement
Hi  I want use swagger to generate client to download file from jenkins artifact api， the data' Content-type from jenkins  is text/plain. 

Now I always get the err like this 

`(*bytes.Buffer) is not supported by the TextConsumer, can be resolved by supporting TextUnmarshaler interface` 



## Swagger specification
/blue/rest/organizations/{organization}/pipelines/{namespace}/pipelines/{namespace}-{name}/runs/{run}/log/:
    get:
      description: download pipeline log
      operationId: downloadPipelineLog
      parameters:
      - description: name of the organization
        in: path

        required: true
        type: string
      - description: namespace of pipelineconfig
        in: path
        name: namespace
        required: true
        type: string
      - description: name of pipelineconfig
        in: path
        name: name
        required: true
        type: string
      - description: name of the run
        in: path
        name: run
        required: true

      - description: start of artifact
        in: query
        name: start
        required: true
        type: integer
      - description: download artifact
        in: query
        name: download
        required: true
        type: boolean
      consumes:
        - application/octet-stream
      produces:
        - application/octet-stream
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successfully get pipeline log file
          schema:
            type: string
            format: binary
          headers:
            Content-Type:
              type: string
            Content-Disposition:
              type: string
        "401":
          description: Authentication failed - incorrect username and/or password
        "403":
          description: Jenkins requires authentication - please set username and password

  

## Steps to reproduce

func (c *Client) GetLogArtifact(ctx context.Context, pipelineNamespace, pipelineName, organization, run string) (*bytes.Buffer, error) {
	downLoadLogParams := operations.
		NewDownloadPipelineLogParams().
		WithContext(ctx).

}



I dont know what I should use for writer in DownloadPipelineLog. because if I use bytes.NewBuffer([]byte{}),  I will always get the err

body:
## Problem statement

I am using go-swagger to generate a server.  In my Swagger document, I define GET and PUT operations with references to an object type defined in the definitions section.  For example:

```
    get:
      summary: Get an object
      responses:
        200:
          description: The object
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/MyObject'
```

The generator creates a Go struct type called *MyObject* in the models directory:

```
// swagger:model MyObject
type MyObject struct {
```

In my server, I load the Swagger spec and create an API from it:

```
	swaggerSpec, err := loads.Embedded(restapi.SwaggerJSON, restapi.FlatSwaggerJSON)
	api := operations.NewObjectAPI(swaggerSpec)
```

I inspect the swaggerSpec.Paths to find a Get operation, and from the info available there, I know that the name of the type it returns is "MyObject".  Unfortunately, Go's reflection doesn't let me resolve the type by its name, so I am hoping that go-swagger does that for me.  I thought that the **formats** property (strfmt.Registry) was the way, but it only seems to have built-in types.

So given a Get operation, how can I determine the Go type that it returns in its 200 OK response?

Thanks.


## Environment
swagger version: v0.21.0
go version: go1.11.5
OS:   Linux
title: How can I resolve the Go Type for an operation that defines an object type?

body:
## Problem statement

Using go-swagger to generate a server.  My schema defines an object with default values specified for some properties, and I am specifying the --strict-additional-properties command-line option.

The generated code has two instances of the UnmarshalJSON() method for this object, resulting in a compilation error.

If the server is generated without the --strict-additional-properties command-line option, only one UnmarshalJSON() method is generated.

In addition, if the default values are removed from the Swagger definition, only one UnmarshalJSON() method is generated.

Please remove the sections that don't apply

## Swagger specification


```
swagger: '2.0'
info:
  title: Test API
  description: Test
  version: v1
host: host
schemes:
- https
- http
basePath: /path
produces:
- application/json

paths:
  /obj:
    get:
      summary: Get an object
      operationId: getObject
      responses:
        200:
          description: The object
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/MyObject'

definitions:
  MyObject:
    title: My object
    type: object
    properties:
      string1:
        type: string
        title: A string
      bool1:
        type: boolean
        title: A boolean
    default:
      string1: "first"
      bool1:   false
```

## Steps to reproduce

1) Generate the server:

```
./go-swagger generate server --strict-additional-properties
```

2) Inspect the resulting models/my_object.go file, and see two definitions for UnmarshalJSON():

```
func (m *MyObject) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error {
	type MyObjectAlias MyObject
	var t MyObjectAlias
	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte("{\"bool1\":false,\"string1\":\"first\"}"), &t); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &t); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	*m = MyObject(t)
	return nil
}

// UnmarshalJSON unmarshals this object while disallowing additional properties from JSON
func (m *MyObject) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
	var props struct {

		// A boolean
		Bool1 bool `json:"bool1,omitempty"`

		// A string
		String1 string `json:"string1,omitempty"`
	}

	dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(data))
	dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
	if err := dec.Decode(&props); err != nil {
		return err
	}

	m.Bool1 = props.Bool1

	m.String1 = props.String1

	return nil
}


```


## Environment
swagger version: v0.21.0 
go version: go1.11.5
OS:   Linux

body:
## Problem statement

Whenever we set up a polymorphic type with the base type having some additionalProperties,
the generated code works by luck only:
- the base type has a method for additionalProperties, but it's commented by some lucky artifact of template space gobbling
- the subtype redefines this method, so it eventually seems to work, although the subtype should be able to inherit this from the base type

I stumbled on that while attempting to clean-up a bit our templates with comments/space gobbling etc...
I've isolated below a minimal case, constructed from the bitbucket canary spec we have in CI.

## Swagger specification
```yaml
swagger: '2.0'
info:
  version: '2.0'
  contact:
    url: 'https://example.org/support'
    name: 'Example Support'
    email: support@example.org
  description: 'Test case inspired by bitbucket API to demonstrate polymorphism with inherited additional properties'
  title: 'test case polymorphism with additional props'
paths:
  '/repositories/component/{id}':
    parameters:
      - required: true
        type: string
        name: id
        in: path
    get:
      responses:
        default:
          description: 'Unexpected component.'
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/component'
  '/repositories/components':
    get:
      responses:
        default:
          description: 'Unexpected component.'
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: '#/definitions/component'
    put:
      parameters:
        - required: true
          in: body
          name: components
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: '#/definitions/component'
      responses:
        default:
          description: 'Unexpected component.'
          schema:
  '/repositories/object/{id}':
    parameters:
      - required: true
        type: string
        name: id
        in: path
    get:
      responses:
        default:
          description: 'Unexpected object.'
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/object'

  '/repositories/object':
    get:
      responses:
        default:
          description: 'Unexpected object.'
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: '#/definitions/object'
    put:
      parameters:
        - required: true
          in: body
          name: components
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: '#/definitions/object'
      responses:
        default:
          description: 'Unexpected component.'
          schema:
schemes:
  - https
basePath: /
produces:
  - application/json
definitions:
  object:
    description: 'Base type for most resource objects. It defines the common `type` element that identifies an object''s type. It also identifies the element as Swagger''s `discriminator`.'
    required:
      - type
    additionalProperties: true
    discriminator: type
    type: object
    properties:
      type:
        type: string
  component:
    allOf:
      -
        $ref: '#/definitions/object'
      -
        additionalProperties: true
        type: object
        description: 'A component as defined in a repository''s issue tracker.'
        properties:
          name:
            type: string
          links:
            additionalProperties: false
            type: object
            properties:
              self:
                additionalProperties: false
                type: object
                properties:
                  href: {type: string, format: uri}
          id:
            type: integer
```

## Steps to reproduce
`swagger generate model -f {spec above}`

## Environment
swagger version: master
go version: x.x.x  
OS:

body:
## Problem statement

Bit of a weird one here; when re-generating multiple API Clients (i.e., from more than one swagger spec), the main client packages all import the same operations package, rather than their own specific operations packages. This only appears to occur as of version `0.22.0`.

(The use case here is a service composed of multiple microservices, each with their own swagger spec. These specs are collated into a single repo where the API Clients are generated. Every time we regenerate the clients the package imports get messed up.)

Will stick with using an older version for now, but any help resolving this would be appreciated!

## Steps to reproduce

Start with an empty directory and setup go mod `go mod init example.com/example-sdk`.

Add the following simple swagger specs to represent our example services, one for 'users' and one for 'articles':

```yaml
# users-spec.yml
swagger: "2.0"
info:
  version: 1.0.0
  title: Users
paths:
  /users:
    get:
      operationId: listUsers
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
          schema:
            type: object
            properties:
              users:
                type: string
```

```yaml
# articles-spec.yml
swagger: "2.0"
info:
  version: 1.0.0
  title: Articles
paths:
  /articles:
    get:
      operationId: listArticles
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
          schema:
            type: object
            properties:
              articles:
                type: string
```

Check you're using current versions of Go and go-swagger:
```shell
$ go version
go version go1.14 darwin/amd64
$ swagger version
version: v0.22.0
commit: 5773cbe63c3f459b23ed73ad8b482389ddf46cb4
```

Then generate an API Client for each service (specifying a client package name to avoid having the second client overwrite the first):

```shell
swagger generate client -c articles -f articles-spec.yml
swagger generate client -c users -f users-spec.yml
```

At this point all seems well with the Articles client:

```go
// articles/articles_client.go
package articles

import (
	"example.com/example-sdk/articles/operations"
	httptransport "github.com/go-openapi/runtime/client"

	"github.com/go-openapi/runtime"
	"github.com/go-openapi/strfmt"
)
```

But if you regenerate it once more (`swagger generate client -c articles -f articles-spec.yml`) it's now importing the _users_ operations package:

```go
// articles/articles_client.go
package articles

import (
	"example.com/example-sdk/users/operations"
	httptransport "github.com/go-openapi/runtime/client"

	"github.com/go-openapi/runtime"
	"github.com/go-openapi/strfmt"
)
```

I've confirmed this doesn't happen with version `0.21.0`, also checked the diff between versions 21 and 22 but can't figure out which change is causing this issue.

Our actual use-case is quite a bit more complex than this example, of course. We have five different services and it appears that, even when deleting the existing API Client files before re-generating, this issue persists, although only in two of the five clients 😕.

## Environment
swagger version: 0.22.0 (installed via homebrew)
go version: 1.14.0
OS: macOS Catalina 10.15.3

body:
## Problem statement

Trying to get a map with non-string keys serialized in a spec does not seem to be working quite consistently. I've read the FAQ entry saying maps non-string keys are not supported in bodies, and #977 -- this is somewhat related to it. Anyway it seems to be supported to some extent after all.

## Swagger specification
```go
package main

type Thing struct {
        Value uint64
}

type Key struct {
        V1 string
        V2 string
}

// swagger:response
type Try1 struct {
        Field1 string
        Field2 map[Key]*Thing
}

// swagger:response
// in: body
type Try2 struct {
        Field1 string
        Field2 map[Key]*Thing
}

// swagger:response
type Try3 struct {
        // in: body
        Field1 string
        // in: body
        Field2 map[Key]*Thing
}

// swagger:response
type Try4 struct {
        // in: body
        Data struct {
                Field1 string
                Field2 map[Key]*Thing
        }
}
```

Produced spec:

```yaml
definitions:
  Thing:
    properties:
      Value:
        format: uint64
        type: integer
    type: object
    x-go-package: _/tmp/x
paths: {}
responses:
  Try1:
    headers:
      Field1:
        type: string
      Field2: {}
    schema:
      additionalProperties:
        $ref: '#/definitions/Thing'
      type: object
  Try2:
    headers:
      Field1:
        type: string
      Field2: {}
    schema:
      additionalProperties:
        $ref: '#/definitions/Thing'
      type: object
  Try3:
    schema:
      additionalProperties:
        $ref: '#/definitions/Thing'
      type: object
  Try4:
    schema:
      properties:
        Field1:
          type: string
        Field2: {}
      type: object
swagger: "2.0"
```

## Steps to reproduce

Try1 is my first simplistic attempt. additionalProperties is in the schema, but Field1 and Field2 end up in Headers.

To overcome the unwanted placement, Try2 adds a "in: body". But that does not have any effect.

Try3 is another attempt to overcome it, by adding "in: body" to both struct members. This makes a difference, but both properties disappear from Headers, without appearing in schema.

Try4 is a different approach, it's more in line with the go-swagger examples. Now Field1 and Field2 are where I like to see them, but additionalProperties have disappeared.

The yaml I'm trying to achieve for this is
```yaml
TryX:
    schema:
      properties:
        Field1:
          type: string
        Field2:
          additionalProperties:
            $ref: '#/definitions/Thing'
          type: object
      type: object
```

Key findings:
- Generating specs for maps with non-string keys appears to be possible in some scenarios after all, as witnessed by Try1, Try2, and Try3 above
- If I change the map key to a string, Try4 produces the result I want. I'd rather not do that though, it would be an unwanted change to the underlying model (which serializes fine through use of Text(Un)Marshaler). Also the extra inner wrapper struct (Data in the Try4 example above) would not be desirable, but I guess I could live with it.

## Environment
swagger version: current git master
go version: 1.14
OS:   Linux

body:
## Problem statement

It's already possible to use a workflow like `swagger generate spec --scan-models` + `swagger generate model --existing-models` to output a full model (`struct`) including its implemented validation methods.

However, if we already start with a `struct` + swagger annotations/comments in the first place and are *only interested in auto-generating its validation methods* (in the same go package as the original `struct` from which we have generated the swagger-spec), we currently have to overwrite templates explicitly (`--allow-template-override --template-dir`) to gain such a workflow. Otherwise the emitted struct and the original struct (same name) collide. 

I propose to add an additional flag to `swagger generate model` called `--only-model-validations` which should effectively fully disable the struct emits in the following templates:
* [/generator/templates/schema.gotmpl: Lines 1-76](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/master/generator/templates/schema.gotmpl#L1-L76)
* [/generator/templates/model.gotmpl: Line 3, 12, 20-21, 23-24](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/master/generator/templates/model.gotmpl)

Is this a workflow this project is interested to support?

## Environment
swagger version: 0.23.0
go version: 1.14.2
OS: Debian

body:
## Problem statement

Object inheritance in Swagger 2.0 is partly supported by `allOf`. It _merges_ all properties of all referenced objects to one. It collides with strict validation of additional properties where every unknown property causes validation failure.

If `additionalProperties: false` is defined in object definition and `--strict-additional-properties` is passed as command line option, JSON object to validate must contain no additional properties. There is `UnmarshalJSON()` validation method generated for such objects. If the object is part of "allOf" construct together with another non-empty object, the validation with `UnmarshalJSON()` will always fails, because incoming JSON structure always contains some fields unknown to marshaller.

I propose two solutions, but there may be other and better, of course:

1. Refactor code generation so that object validation to unknown properties would be in `UnmarshalJSON()` method of `allOf` object (`DerivedProperty` in the example) and not of referenced object (`BaseProperty` in the example). The generator would have to be able to distinguish if the object requires validation for unknown properties, otherwise it would generate code for usual demarshalling.
1. Introduce a boolean field to every generated Go struct that would specify if to perform validation or not. `UnmarshalJSON()` method would perform validation according to the field.

I see some problem that there are several different `UnmarshalJSON()` instances in generator templates which can cause confusion and incompatibility between them (see #2189). Also notice that `allOf` definition is not validated to strict additional properties.

## Swagger specification

```
swagger: '2.0'
info:
  title: Test strict additional properties of object referenced in "allOf" 
  version: 0.0.1
schemes:
  - http
consumes:
  - application/json
produces:
  - application/json
paths:
  /test:
    post:
      summary: Base/derived object test
      description: Validation of strict additional properties in base object
      parameters:
      - name: body
        in: body
        description: Object body parameter
        required: true
        schema:
          $ref: '#/definitions/DerivedProperty'
        x-nullable: false
      responses:
        200:
          description: Successful response
        default:
          description: Failure
definitions:
  BaseProperty:
    type: object
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
        enum:
          - ConstantName
        x-nullable: false
    x-nullable: false
    additionalProperties: false
  DerivedProperty:
    type: object
    allOf:
    - $ref: '#/definitions/BaseProperty'
    - type: object
      properties:
        value:
          type: string
          enum:
            - ConstantValue
          x-nullable: false
      x-nullable: false
      additionalProperties: false
    x-nullable: false
    additionalProperties: false
```

## Steps to reproduce

Execute Swagger generator with forbidden additional properties:

```
swagger generate server -f no-additional-properties-in-inheritance.yaml --strict-additional-properties
```

It generates validation function for `BaseProperty`:

```
// UnmarshalJSON unmarshals this object while disallowing additional properties from JSON
func (m *BaseProperty) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
	var props struct {

		// name
		// Enum: [ConstantName]
		Name string `json:"name,omitempty"`
	}

	dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(data))
	dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
	if err := dec.Decode(&props); err != nil {
		return err
	}

	m.Name = props.Name
	return nil
}
```

and for `DerivedProperty` too:

```
// UnmarshalJSON unmarshals this object from a JSON structure
func (m *DerivedProperty) UnmarshalJSON(raw []byte) error {
	// AO0
	var aO0 BaseProperty
	if err := swag.ReadJSON(raw, &aO0); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	m.BaseProperty = aO0

	// AO1
	var dataAO1 struct {
		Value string `json:"value,omitempty"`
	}
	if err := swag.ReadJSON(raw, &dataAO1); err != nil {
		return err
	}

	m.Value = dataAO1.Value

	return nil
}
```

When the server receives some JSON data, for example:

```
{
  "name": "ConstantName",
  "value": "ConstantValue"
}
```

call of `dec.Decode()` fails, because `BaseProperty` recognizes property `name` only and additional properties (`value` in this case) are forbidden with `DisallowUnknownFields()`, introduced in #2040.

## Environment

swagger version: v0.23.0 (2020-03-14)
go version: go1.13.3 linux/amd64
OS: Ubuntu 19.10

labels.name: model
labels.description: Related to swagger generate model command

title: Validation failure when object with forbidden additional properties is referenced in "allOf"

body:
## Problem statement
Client code generation creates code which fails when used, with the following JSON error:

```
json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []*models.Hello
```

## Swagger specification

```
basePath: /
consumes:
- application/json
definitions:
  Hello:
    properties:
      message:
        $ref: '#/definitions/Message'
    type: object
    x-go-package: github.com/glindsell/hello/api
  Message:
    properties:
      words:
        type: string
        x-go-name: Body
    type: object
    x-go-package: github.com/glindsell/hello/api
host: localhost:8080
info:
  description: Documentation of our hello API.
  title: hello.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /hello:
    post:
      consumes:
      - application/json
      operationId: idOfHelloEndpoint
      parameters:
      - in: query
        name: name
        type: string
        x-go-name: Name
      produces:
      - application/json
      responses:
        "200":
          $ref: '#/responses/helloResponse'
      summary: Hello returns hello world string with given input.
      tags:
      - hello-tag
produces:
- application/json
responses:
  helloResponse:
    description: This text will appear as description of your response body.
    schema:
      items:
        $ref: '#/definitions/Hello'
      type: array
schemes:
- http
swagger: "2.0"
```

## Code example

A contrived set of parameter and response structs to illustrate the server code producing this error:

```
// HelloRequest represents body of Hello request.
// swagger:parameters idOfHelloEndpoint
type HelloParamsWrapper struct {
        // in: query
        Name string `json:"name"`
}

// This text will appear as description of your response body.
// swagger:response helloResponse
type HelloResponse struct {
        // in:body
        Payload []*Hello `json:"body,omitempty"`
}

type Hello struct {
        Message *Message `json:"message"`
}

type Message struct {
        Body string `json:"words"`
}
```

## Client app output

Example of output from app using generated client code (DEBUG=1):

```
POST /hello?name=George HTTP/1.1

{"body":[{"message":{"words":"Hello George"}}]}
2020/04/23 13:16:18 error fetching hello: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []*models.Hello
exit status 1
```

body:
## Problem statement

I specify a definition to be of type object with additionalProperties: true. I expect the generated client model to be of type map[string]interface{}. I see the resulting model instead has type interface{}

I would like to have a model generated as a type of map[string]interface{}, as I would like to define member functions on top of this model and cannot if it is of type interface{}

I have tried with 0.22, 0.23, and 0.17, but I continue to see this issue.

While changing it to
```
type: "object"
additionalProperties:
  type: "object"
```
does generate a map[string]interface{}, this is incompatible with the server definition as it is using javascript's swagger and will expect the values of each key to themselves be JSON, but my target is to have the values be any type. So I would like `additionalProperties: true` to generate map[string]interface{} so the client and server do not have contention issues.

I expected the behavior of map[string]interface{} given the following resources:
The documentation suggests this is the way to do it: https://goswagger.io/use/models/schemas.html (Ctrl+F for "additionalProperties: true", and it has an example suggesting that the addition of this causes the generated code to have the type map[string]interface{})
https://goswagger.io/faq/faq_model.html (Also says that if I add additionalProperties: true it will generate a map[string]interface{})

In addition, the following tickets all suggest this is a valid method of achieving the desired result:
https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/issues/1450#issuecomment-385613822 (not stated outright but that is how I understood this comment)
https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/issues/2081#issuecomment-541296380


## Swagger specification
```
swagger: "2.0"
info:
  version: "1.0.0"
  title: "Swagger Petstore"
host: "petstore.swagger.io"
basePath: "/v2"
paths:
  /pet:
    post:
      operationId: "addPet"
      parameters:
      - in: "body"
        name: "body"
        schema:
          $ref: "#/definitions/Pet"
      responses:
        "405":
          description: "Invalid input"
definitions:
  Pet:
    type: "object"
    additionalProperties: true
```
## Steps to reproduce
```
$ go-swagger generate client -t <outputDir> -f <aboveSnippet>
$ cat <outputDir>/models/pet.go
// Code generated by go-swagger; DO NOT EDIT.

package models

// This file was generated by the swagger tool.
// Editing this file might prove futile when you re-run the swagger generate command

// Pet pet
// swagger:model Pet
type Pet interface{}
```

I expect that since the Pet definition has `additionalProperties: true`, that the resulting model would be `type Pet map[string]interface{}`, not `type Pet interface{}`

## Environment
swagger version: 0.22.0  
go version: 1.13.1  
OS:  MacOS High Sierra/Darwin

body:
## Problem statement

I am trying to implement an API that returns a file (in text/csv format) for 200 OK, and JSON for errors.  I have successfully implemented the file for the 200 OK response using a custom middleware.ResponderFunc().  But when I try to return a 400 error, I get this:

```
2020/05/06 11:01:29 http: panic serving 127.0.0.1:58012: csv producer has not yet been implemented
```

The server believes that I want it to send the response object in CSV format, but I don't; I want to send it in JSON format, as I do every other error in every other API in my swagger. 

Please remove the sections that don't apply

## Swagger specification

At the top level, I produce JSON:

```yaml
produces:
- application/json
```

In this API, I want to produce text/csv for the 200 OK response, and JSON for the other responses.  I have tried this:

```yaml
  /csv:
    get:
      summary: Get some csv
      operationId: getCCsv

      produces:
        - application/json
        - text/csv

      responses:
        200:
          description: CSV file to download
          schema:
            type: file
          headers:
            Content-Disposition:
              type: string
              description: A header specifying the file name
        400:
          description: Bad request
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/APIError'
```

When the request is correct, everything works.  But when the request is invalid, and I try to return a 400 response, I get the panic specified above:

```go
		// We need to return an error
		error := models.APIError{
			Code:       responseCode,
			Message:    err.Error(),
		}

		// Which object?
		switch responseCode {
		case 400:
			return cdrs.NewGetCdrsBadRequest().WithPayload(&error)
```

The only way I can get it to work is to remove the "text/csv" from all "produces:".  But that makes my swagger a lie; it claims that the 200 OK response will be application/json, but it is not; it is text/csv.

How can I tell the generated server that the 200 OK response produces text/csv, but the other responses produce application/JSON?

Thanks.


## Environment
swagger version: v0.23.0 
go version: go1.11.5
OS:   Ubuntu Linux 16.x
title: "csv producer has not yet been implemented" on error

body:
## Problem statement
I'm unable to generate the Swagger specification for making more than one security header required for authentication. For example, I'm using `x_client_id` and `access_token` for authenticating the request, but I'm not able to generate the Swagger specs for it.

The issues [here](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/issues/1089) mentions that the go swagger has the support for **AND** logic in the Security headers, but I'm not able to find the documentation for the same.

I'm using the following comments for generating the Swagger specs:-
```
//  security:
//   - x_client_id:
//      access_token:
//
//  SecurityDefinitions:
//  x_client_id:
//    type: apiKey
//    name: x_client_id
//    in: header
//  access_token:
//    type: apiKey
//    name: Authorization
//    in: header
```

It generates the following Swagger specs:-
```
security:
- x_client_id: []
- access_token: []
securityDefinitions:
  access_token:
    in: header
    name: Authorization
    type: apiKey
  x_client_id:
    in: header
    name: x_client_id
    type: apiKey
```

The expected output should be:- 
```
security:
- x_client_id: []
  access_token: []
securityDefinitions:
  access_token:
    in: header
    name: Authorization
    type: apiKey
  x_client_id:
    description: Use the client id provided by the API
    in: header
    name: x_client_id
    type: apiKey
```

## Environment
swagger version: 2.0
go version: go1.14.2
OS: macOS 10.14.5

body:
## Problem statement

swagger validate returns `WARNING: definition [...] is not used anywhere` if a definition is used in composition using `allOf`.

Is this the expected behaviour?
We are getting more strict against warnings in our CI and as far as I understood this is the right way to do things. But then why there is this warning?


## Swagger specification

```yaml
swagger: "2.0"

info:
  title: Sample API
  description: 'API description in Markdown.'
  version: '1.0.0'

host: 'api.example.com'

basePath: '/v1'

schemes:
  - https
    
paths:
  /pets:
    get:
      summary: Returns pets.
      description: Optional extended description in Markdown.
      produces:
        - application/json
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
          schema:
            type: object
            required: 
              - data
            properties:
              data:
                $ref: '#/definitions/Pet'


definitions:
  Pet:
    type: object
    required:
      - pet_type
    properties:
      pet_type:
        type: string
    discriminator: pet_type
  Dog:     # "Dog" is a value for the pet_type property (the discriminator value)
    allOf: # Combines the main `Pet` schema with `Dog`-specific properties 
      - $ref: '#/definitions/Pet'
      - type: object
        # all other properties specific to a `Dog`
        properties:
          bark:
            type: boolean
          breed:
            type: string
            enum: [Dingo, Husky, Retriever, Shepherd]
  Cat:     # "Cat" is a value for the pet_type property (the discriminator value)
    allOf: # Combines the main `Pet` schema with `Cat`-specific properties 
      - $ref: '#/definitions/Pet'
      - type: object
        # all other properties specific to a `Cat`
        properties:
          hunts:
            type: boolean
          age:
            type: integer
```

## Steps to reproduce

Run `swagger validate` on a file containing the specs above.

It gives two warnings:
```
2020/06/08 13:52:07 - WARNING: definition "#/definitions/Dog" is not used anywhere
2020/06/08 13:52:07 - WARNING: definition "#/definitions/Cat" is not used anywhere
```

## Environment
swagger version: v0.23.0


go version: go1.12.17 darwin/amd64

OS: MacOS 10.15.4

## Problem statement

I have forked go-swagger/go-swagger in order to customize the templates used to generate the server.  I can't convince dep to check out the go-swagger/generator/templates directory (and subdirectories) into my vendor directory, because it contains no Go files.

I have added this to my Gopkg.toml file:

```
required = [
    "github.com/mycompany.com/go-swagger",
    "github.com/mycompany.com/go-swagger/generator",
    "github.com/mycompany.com/go-swagger/generator/templates"
]
```

But when I run "dep ensure", I get this error:

```
Solving failure: No versions of github.com/mycompany.com/go-swagger met constraints:
	v0.23.0: Could not introduce github.com/mycompany.com/go-swagger@v0.23.0, as its subpackage github.com/mycompany.com/go-swagger/generator/templates does not contain usable Go code (*build.NoGoError).. (Package is required by (root).)
```

What is the best way to add the templates to my git repository?

Thanks.

body:
## Problem statement

When generating models with `swagger generate model` or `swagger generate server` the resulting models from the swagger definitions all implement a validation method like `Validate(formats strfmt.Registry) error`. However, the generated models for parameters do not include this functionality.

Validations for the parameter are currently generated but only used while binding the param struct. In order to use the validations standalone, without having to bind them with the generated method (for example when binding with a different framework), I propose to add a `Validate(formats strfmt.Registry) error` method to https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/548768a026b86c7f9836e1c0dc76cbdce3718767/generator/templates/server/parameter.gotmpl#L489 to be able to use the parameter validation separately.

Is there any interest in adding this functionality?

## Environment
swagger version: 0.23.0  
go version: 1.14.2  
OS: Debian
title: No Validate method for server parameter structs

body:
## Problem statement

The [docs](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/a4cab2f27a8537cd8dd9de426eece245f4ab35e1/docs/use/spec/ignore.md#swaggerignore) say that `swagger:ignore` removes structs from the swagger spec output. Since #1497 it also applies to struct fields. I was trying to hide a field and failed to find the answer in the docs, after Googling I found the issue and realised ignore was indeed what I wanted.

I think the docs should also be updated to include the new behaviour.

body:
I need to write sort of middleware that works on responses. Implementing it as standard HTTP middleware is inconvenient since all you have is ResponseWriter and must record everything via httptest.ResponseRecorder and then work on raw data. Its much easier to work on implementations/interfaces that are returned from handlers as middleware.Responder.

I propose adding access methods for all attributes of generated implementations of middleware.Responder. First and foremost for status code of generated errors, but all other attributes will be helpful too.
title: Add access methods for attributes of generated implementations of middleware.Responder

I would like to add a domain-specific custom format to my swagger spec and generate corresponding code. Based on the existing `RegisterFormat` method on the generated api, I would expect I can do this:

```go
api := operations.NewMyAPI(spec)
api.RegisterFormat("custom", customFormatter, customValidator)
```

and use it in my spec like this:

```yaml
definitions:
  CustomObject:
    type: object
    properties:
      customField:
        type: string
        format: custom
```

but this doesn't work because the allowed list of formats used in the generator is hardcoded. So my custom format will never make it to the generated model.

---

Does that mean that `RegisterFormat` is only useful for types that I provide using `x-go-type`? I found issue #732 hinting that providing custom formats has not been fully implemented. Is that the case or am I missing something?

## Problem statement

A specification that includes a response with an array of objects can be used successfully to generate server stubs.  However, a spec cannot then be generated from the resulting server.  I did not find a way to modify the server code or tags to enable the "generate spec" command to succeed.

The issue is reproducible with the example at https://goswagger.io/tutorial/todo-list.html and with the simple case below.

## Swagger specification

```
swagger: '2.0'
info:
  description: |-
    Test
  version: 1.0.0
  title: test
consumes:
- application/json
produces:
- application/json
paths:
  /:
    get:
      parameters: [
        ]
      responses:
        200:
          description: Success
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: '#/definitions/Result'
definitions:
  Result:
    type: object
    properties:
      id:
        type: string
        readOnly: true
      name:
        type: string
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Copy the spec above into foo.yaml.
1. swagger generate server -A foo -f [...]/foo.yaml -t [...]/foo
1. swagger generate spec -o [...]/foo/foo-regen.yaml

The generate spec command produces this DEBUG output:

When the spec is changed to return a single object, the reverse generation is successful.  I.e., change this:

Read Only: true

body:
## Problem statement

Server returns `501: operation PostResource has not yet been implemented` response despite the fact that the handler is indeed implemented.

## Technical details

This is caused by the `if-handler-is-nil` check in the initially auto-generated `restapi/configure_service.go` file (specifically the `configureAPI` function):
```
func configureAPI(api *operations.ServiceAPI) http.Handler {
	// ...

	if api.PostResourceHandler == nil {
		api.PostResourceHandler = operations.PostResourceHandlerFunc(func(params operations.PostResourceParams) middleware.Responder {
			return handlers.PostResource(params)
	})

	// ...
}
```

The `main()` function in `cmd/service-server/main.go` calls `api := operations.NewServiceAPI` prior to calling `server.ConfigureServiceAPI`.

`operations.NewServiceAPI` generates a `ServiceAPI` object with *non-nil handlers* that return a `middleware.NotImplemented("operation PostResource has not yet been implemented")` response. Since each of these handlers are not nil, they are never configured in the `configureAPI` function in `restapi/configure_service.go`, so the server always returns the `501: operation PostResource has not yet been implemented` response.

## Swagger specification

```
consumes:
- application/json
info:
  title: A sample API
  version: 0.0.0
basePath: /v0
produces:
- application/json
schemes:
- http
swagger: "2.0"

paths:
  /participants:
    post:
      operationId: post_resource
      summary: Add a resource
      parameters:
        - name: resource
          in: body
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/Resource'
            example:
              code: 1
      responses:
        202:
        	description: Resource accepted

definitions:
  Resource:
    type: object
    required:
      - code
    properties: 
      code:
        type: integer
        description: An integer code
        example: 1
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. In an empty folder, write a `swagger.yaml` spec with an endpoint specified in `paths`. The swagger specification above is sufficient.
2. Write a handler for the endpoint and place it in the `configureAPI` function in the `restapi/configure_service.go` file. The handler could just print a "hello world", return a `202 Accepted`, etc. Example:
```
if api.PostResourceHandler == nil {
		api.PostResourceHandler = operations.PostResourceHandlerFunc(func(params operations.PostResourceParams) middleware.Responder {
			fmt.Println("Handling the Resource...")
			return operations.NewPostResourceAccepted()
	})
}
```
3. Generate the server with: `swagger generate server -A service swagger.yaml`
4. Build with: `go build -o main cmd/service-server/main.go`
5. Run the server with `./main --port=3000`
6. Send a valid request to the endpoint.

The expected result would be whatever behaviour you set the handler to carry out. Instead, I am seeing a `501: operation PostResource has not yet been implemented` response.

## Environment

swagger versions: observed in 0.24.0 and 0.25.0
go version: 1.14  
OS: Ubuntu 20.04

## Quick fix

If anyone else is having a similar problem, simply remove the `if-handler-is-nil` check for all of your endpoints.

ie. For every endpoint, replace the following code block in the `configureAPI` function in the `restapi/configure_service.go` file:
```
if api.PostResourceHandler == nil {
		api.PostResourceHandler = operations.PostResourceHandlerFunc(func(params operations.PostResourceParams) middleware.Responder {
			fmt.Println("Handling the Resource...")
			return operations.NewPostResourceAccepted()
	})
}
```
with this block:
```
api.PostResourceHandler = operations.PostResourceHandlerFunc(func(params operations.PostResourceParams) middleware.Responder {
			fmt.Println("Handling the Resource...")
			return operations.NewPostResourceAccepted()
})
```
title: Auto-generated API configuration file causes inappropriate "501: Not Implemented" responses

body:
## Problem statement

`go-swagger` allows `null` values for array items where it shouldn't. When defining an array having items that result in Go pointers, `go-swagger` inserts a special case to skip validation for nil pointers.

## Swagger specification

```yaml
consumes:
  - application/json
info:
  title: Foo
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /foo:
    put:
      parameters:
        - in: body
          name: foo
          required: true
          schema:
            items:
              properties:
                foo:
                  type: boolean
              type: object
            type: array
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
swagger: "2.0"
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Generate a new server with the specification given above.
2. Run the server.
3. Perform a `PUT` request to the `/foo` endpoint with `[null]` as body and observe that validation succeeds.

## Environment
swagger version: v0.25.0
go version: go1.13
OS: darwin

## Problem statement

Hi,

First of all, thanks for your great work in `go-swagger`. I've been using it over the past months and I really had a great time. However I'm currently trying to annotate structures with `Extension`-related stuff and can not get it to work. I actually found this issue which might be related but not sure if it is still relevant https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/issues/2106.

My goal is to get a given structure to be imported from its original package. In `yaml` spec, it gives us:

```yaml
MyType:
  x-go-type:
    import:
      package: github.com/org/repo/pkg/name
    type: MyType
```

However, if I try the following:

```go
// swagger:model
// Extensions:
// ---
// x-go-type:
//   import:
//     package: github.com/org/repo/pkg/name
//   type: MyType
// ---
type MyType struct {
	ID uuid.UUID `json:"id"`
}
```

And then run `swagger generate spec --include=name -m -o swagger.yaml`, what I get is the following:

```yaml
  MyType:
    properties:
      id:
        $ref: '#/definitions/UUID'
    type: object
    x-go-package: github.com/org/repo/pkg/name
```

Is it something that is kind of expected ? I am missing something here ?

Thanks in advance

## Environment
swagger version: v0.25.0
go version: go1.15.2 darwin/amd64
OS: macOS

body:
## Problem statement

Using `discriminator` with `allOf` creates invalid Go code (embedded struct in an interface and missing interface member functions)

## Swagger specification

```
---
swagger: '2.0'
info:
  version: 0.0.1
  title: example
  contact: { }
host: example.com
basePath: /
schemes:
  - https
consumes:
  - application/json
produces:
  - application/json
paths:
  '/example':
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          $ref: '#/responses/exampleResponse'
responses:
  exampleResponse:
    description: Example
    schema:
      $ref: '#/definitions/parentType'
definitions:
  base:
    type: object
    properties:
      id:
        type: string
  parentType:
    discriminator: type
    allOf:
      - $ref: '#/definitions/base'
      - type: object
        properties:
          parentProp:
            type: string
          type:
            type: string

  childType:
    allOf:
      - $ref: '#/definitions/parentType'
      - type: object
        properties:
          childProp:
            type: string

```

## Steps to reproduce

- Use the above swagger spec
- Run `swagger generate server`
- look at `parent_type.go`
- See:

```
// ParentType parent type
//
// swagger:discriminator parentType type
type ParentType interface {
	runtime.Validatable

	Base //invalid, struct in an interface

	// parent prop
	ParentProp() string
	SetParentProp(string)

	// type
	Type() string
	SetType(string)

	// AdditionalProperties in base type should be handled just like regular properties
	// At this moment, the base type property is pushed down to the subtype
}
```

and

```
func unmarshalParentType(data []byte, consumer runtime.Consumer) (ParentType, error) {
	buf := bytes.NewBuffer(data)
	buf2 := bytes.NewBuffer(data)

	// the first time this is read is to fetch the value of the type property.
	var getType struct {
		Type string `json:"type"`
	}
	if err := consumer.Consume(buf, &getType); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	if err := validate.RequiredString("type", "body", getType.Type); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	// The value of type is used to determine which type to create and unmarshal the data into
	switch getType.Type {
	case "childType":
		var result ChildType
		if err := consumer.Consume(buf2, &result); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		return &result, nil  //invalid, does not satisfy interface
	case "parentType":
		var result parentType
		if err := consumer.Consume(buf2, &result); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		return &result, nil //invalid, does not satisfy interface
	}
	ret
```

## Environment
swagger version: 
version: v0.25.0
commit: f032690aab0634d97e2861a708d8fd9365ba77d2

go version: go version go1.14.7 windows/amd64
OS: Win10

body:
## Problem statement

I am trying to define an enumerated integer type with well-named constants using the **x-go-const-names:** property.  I can't get the generated type to include any constants for integer enums.

## Swagger specification

```
  EventType:
    type: integer
    format: uint32
    description: Event Type
    enum:
      - 1
      - 2
      - 3

    x-go-const-names:
      - One
      - Two
      - Three
```

## Steps to reproduce

Generate models with the above definition.  The resulting type is as expected:

```
// EventType Event Type
//
// swagger:model eventType
type EventType uint32
```

But there are no Go constants defined for EventTypeOne, EventTypeTwo, or EventTypeThree.  In fact, there are no constants defined with any name at all.

If I change the type to string, and change the enum values to "1", "2", and "3", I do get constants defined, but without the x-go-const-names values I specified:

```
const (

	// EventTypeNr1 captures enum value "1"
	EventTypeNr1 EventType = "1"

	// EventTypeNr2 captures enum value "2"
	EventTypeNr2 EventType = "2"

	// EventTypeNr3 captures enum value "3"
	EventTypeNr3 EventType = "3"
)
```

For my integer enum, I would expect to see something like this:

```
const (

	// EventTypeOne captures enum value 1
	EventTypeOne EventType = 1

	// EventTypeTwo captures enum value 2
	EventTypeTwo EventType = 2

	// EventTypeThree captures enum value 3
	EventTypeThree EventType = 3
)
```

But instead, there are no constants defined at all.

How can I generate my desired constants for an integer enum?

Thanks.

## Environment
swagger version: v0.23.0
go version: 1.12.17
OS:   Ubuntu 18

body:
## Problem statement

Like shown in below specs (i.e. Swagger specification section), I'm trying to reuse the `privacy_options` definition, but when I do this, the generated go code doesn't have custom struct tag (below is the generated code)
```
type Privacy struct {

	// mobile number
	MobileNumber PrivacyOptions `json:"mobile_number,omitempty"`

	// name
	Name PrivacyOptions `json:"name,omitempty"`
}
```

As a workaround, I need to duplicate the definitions to make the custom tag work (like in below snippet), which I'm trying to avoid. Could you please suggest if there is a way to have reusability and also have the custom tags work? Please let me know if you are not able to reproduce the issue.

```
  privacy:
    title: Privacy settings
    type: object
    x-go-custom-tag: 'firestore:"privacy_setting"'
    properties:
      name:
        x-go-custom-tag: 'firestore:"name"'
        type: integer
        enum:
          - 1
          - 2
          - 3
      mobile_number:
        x-go-custom-tag: 'firestore:"mobile_number"'
        type: integer
        enum:
          - 1
          - 2
          - 3
```

With this workaround, the code generated is as I'm expecting (see below)
```
type Privacy struct {

	// mobile number
	// Enum: [1 2 3]
	MobileNumber int64 `json:"mobile_number,omitempty" firestore:"mobile_number"`

	// name
	// Enum: [1 2 3]
	Name int64 `json:"name,omitempty" firestore:"name"`
}
```

## Swagger specification
```
swagger: "2.0"
info:
  title: ""
  version: "0.0.1"
schemes:
  - http

paths:
  /api/userprofile:
    post:
      summary: "posts the user profile details into db"
      parameters:
        - in: body
          name: profiles
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: "#/definitions/profile"
      responses:
        200:
          description: "returns the posted user or users"
          schema:
            type: array
            items:
              $ref: "#/definitions/profile"

definitions:
  profile:
    title: profile
    type: object
    properties:
      profile_id:
        description: unique identification of user profile
        type: string
        x-go-custom-tag: 'firestore:"profile_id"'
      privacy_setting:
        $ref: "#/definitions/privacy"

  # nested definitions
  privacy:
    title: Privacy settings
    type: object
    x-go-custom-tag: 'firestore:"privacy_setting"'
    properties:
      name:
        x-go-custom-tag: 'firestore:"name"'
        $ref: "#/definitions/privacy_options"
      mobile_number:
        x-go-custom-tag: 'firestore:"mobile_number"'
        $ref: "#/definitions/privacy_options"
  privacy_options:
    type: integer
    enum:
      - 1
      - 2
      - 3
```

## Steps to reproduce
1. save the above specs to swagger.yml 
2. create empty `gen` folder
3. run below swagger command
```
swagger generate server \
	--target=./gen \
	--spec=./swagger.yml \
	--exclude-main \
	--name=myapp
```

## Environment
swagger version: `dev` 
go version: `1.14.2 `
OS: `macOS Mojave v 10.14.6`

body:
## Problem statement

go-swagger handles `$ref` paths differently in flatten and validate mode.

## Swagger specification

[test-spec.zip](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/files/5760870/test-spec.zip)

## Environment
swagger version: 0.25.0  
go version: 1.14.2  
OS: Windows

## Steps to reproduce

I am using recommended docker mode to run go-swagger, setting concrete version 0.25.0 (see `swagger.cmd`) via cmd file.

I have my spec divided into files for every path and every model, in `paths` and `models` subdirectories respectively. I also have a root file `swagger.yaml` to bring all the paths together and another file `pathParams.yaml` to reuse RESTful path parameters for all types of items IDs.

All those files are located in `swagger` subdirectory of my project. If I try to validate my spec, I got:

```
C:\Users\Me\app\swagger>swagger validate swagger.yaml

The swagger spec at "swagger.yaml" is invalid against swagger specification 2.0.
See errors below:
- some references could not be resolved in spec. First found: open /app/swagger/paths/models/Error.yaml: no such file or directory
```

If I generate flattened json spec, it generates and validates correctly:

```
C:\Users\Me\app\swagger>swagger flatten swagger.yaml -o swagger.json
<no output, file is created>

C:\Users\Me\app\swagger>swagger validate swagger.json
2021/01/02 21:19:12
The swagger spec at "swagger.json" is valid against swagger specification 2.0
```

So it seems to me that go-swagger handles $ref paths differently in flatten and validate modes. I want to validate my spec as a part of CI pipeline and don't want to generate extra artifacts.

Moreover, when I try to use `github.com/go-openapi/validate` package to validate my app output against the spec - I got the "file not found" error while calling `loads.Spec() / analysis.Flatten()`. I hope solving that problem by understanding roots of this issue.

body:
## Problem statement

I have generated a swagger specification from annotated go code (with `swagger generate spec`). However, I miss how to validate the API input in runtime to enforce that the input comply with the schema.
I considered 2 approaches:
a) Include validator tags in the go structs. However, these validator tags are ignored by the spec generator. I wouldn't like to work in parallel in the validation tags and the annotated go code because it is error prone and it is probably that they finally differ.
b) Read the json schemas from the spec and apply the appropriate json schema to the input. In this case, I'm not sure how to process the spec to read the json schemas.

I guess that if I start from the swagger spec and I generate the server code, it will include this validators. However, I would prefer to go in the opposite direction: I develop the code and the tool generates the swagger spec.

Is there any recommendation to validate the model of an API request when using `swagger generate spec`?

## Swagger specification

This is just a fragment of the swagger specification generate from the annotated code:

```
definitions:
  UserUpdateBody:
    properties:
      name:
        minLength: 1
        type: string
        x-go-name: Name
    required:
    - name
    type: object
    x-go-package: github.com/...
paths:
  /users/{userID}:
    put:
      operationId: updateUser
      parameters:
      - description: User identifier
        in: path
        name: userID
        required: true
        type: string
        x-go-name: UserID
      - description: User information to create or update a user
        in: body
        name: Body
        required: true
        schema:
          $ref: '#/definitions/UserUpdateBody'
      responses:
        "200":
          $ref: '#/responses/userInfoResponse'
swagger: "2.0"
```

I would like that if I send a request to updateUser operation with a JSON body that does not contain the required parameter `name` or if `name` is empty, the server generates a 400 error response with information about the invalid request. Although I could create the code or middleware to perform the validation, I'm not sure how to validate it using the JSON schema of swagger spec.

## Problem statement
I have been on an older version of go swagger for a while (0.20). After upgrading to a newer version, I noticed that some values that were values became pointers, with no other changes to the spec. Looking through the issues, I found this other issues where this problem is mentioned briefly by robert1988: https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/issues/2482 

According to him this problem happened when upgrading to 0.26. What I would like to know is how to change it back?


## Swagger specification
Here is an example specification. Note that presetEnum is an enum, that is required, and x-nullable: false is being used to generate a value instead of a pointer. Before 0.26, this spec generates a value, now it generates a pointer. Why is x-nullable: false being ignored?

```
  presetEnum:
    type: string
    default: yup
    enum:
      - yup
      - an
      - enum

  exampleRequest:
    type: object
    required:
      - presetEnum

        $ref: '#/definitions/presetEnum'
        x-nullable: false
```

body:
## Problem statement

I want to validate that a string has a max length of bytes.
As far as I know that validation doesn't exist.

I followed FAQ recommendation about "repetitive customization"
> Further, for repetitive customization, you might be willing to customize the generator's templates. Like in [here](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/master/generator/templates/schemavalidator.gotmpl) for models, or in [here](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/master/generator/templates/server/parameter.gotmpl) for inline parameters.

I have two questions:
1. Can I achieve my goal without **overriding** the full **schemavalidator.gotmpl** file?

body:
## Problem Statement

So when generating a client, as shown [here](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/master/examples/todo-list/client/todos/add_one_parameters.go#L29) the default client timeout is set to `cr.DefaultTimeout` which is `30 seconds`.
So what would be the easiest solution to change that default timeout for all my requests, for example if I want to change that default to `5 seconds`.

Of course I can change the timeout for each request by using `WithTimeout` constructor or `SetTimeout` method but what I want is to change the default, so I don't have to manually change it for each request.

## Possible Solution That Could Be Implemented
If this is not possible currently (did not find anything in the docs), a possible solution would to allow the user to set the default timeout with a CLI argument that the user optionally sets when generating a client. 

For example:

```
swagger generate client --default-timeout-seconds=5 ...
```

And then in the params structs we see:

```go
func NewAddOneParams() *AddOneParams {
	return &AddOneParams{
		timeout: 5 * time.Second,
	}
}
```



## Environment
swagger version: 0.26.1
go version: 1.16.2 
OS: macOS 10.15.7
title: How to change the default timeout in generated client

Hello,

I would like to support `Accept-Encoding: gzip` (client) and `Content-Encoding: gzip` (server) so that if the client sets the header, the server will return a compressed json as response and if the client does not specify it, the server returns the uncompressed json. So I added `Accept-Encoding` as header parameter in the openAPI spec and continued to use `application/json` for consume. I used the generated client to send the request and receive the response. But for the response I run into the error `invalid character '\x1f' looking for beginning of value` when I set `Accept-Encoding: gzip` in my request. Is there anything I can do to support this?

body:
## Problem statement

Per [RFC-6750 section 3](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6750#section-3), OAuth2 unauthenticated responses are required to have a WWW-Authenticate header:

_If the protected resource request does not include authentication credentials or does not contain an access token that enables access to the protected resource, the resource server MUST include the HTTP "WWW-Authenticate" response header field; it MAY include it in response to other conditions as well._

It appears that there's no supported way to do this in the go-swagger authentication. A workaround could be to manually set headers on the authentication response, but I haven't found a way to do so.

## Swagger specification

```
securityDefinitions:
  OAuth2:
    type: oauth2
    flow: accessCode
    authorizationUrl: https://example.com/oauth/authorize
    tokenUrl: https://example.com/oauth/token
    scopes:
      ex:app:admin: Admin access
      ex:app:read: Read access
      ex:app:write: Write access
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Create an endpoint protected by an oauth2 security definition
2. Return an error 400, 401, or 403 from the authentication function
3. Check for presence of a WWW-Authenticate header

## Environment
swagger version: 0.27.0  
go version: go1.16.6  
OS: darwin/amd64

labels.name: auth
labels.description: API authentication

title: OAuth2 authentication doesn't support WWW-Authenticate header

body:
## Problem statement

Empty query or formData parameters are incorrectly treated as missing parameters. I wanted to specify a PATCH RESTful API which would allow modifying my entity by both changing the field and deleting it. Where deleting a field would be devoted by sending an empty key. Unfortunately with current `go-swagger` it's not possible to implement such an API. The reason being that both query parameter handling path and formData handling path change (alias) empty parameter to a missing parameter along the parameter parsing way.

The consequence of this handling is not just the fact that empty query and formData parameters cannot be differentiated, but also that some validation modifiers of OpenAPI2 are then pointless. For example let's think of query or formData parameter of type `string`. In such a case specifying `minLength: 1` modifier never has any effect. The parameter is either empty and then it's considered to be missing by the validation code -> `minLength` doesn't apply, or it's non empty and then `minLength: 1` check always passes. Another example might be that for `string`, `required: true` implies `minLength: 1`.

After reading RFCs I have concluded that both query and formDara parameters of HTTP call are formed by multiple `<key>=<value>` statements. Then statement `foo=` is valid statement in both query and formData which simply specifies an empty value. Go `http` package also reflects this logic and provides methods which allows to differentiate empty and missing query and formData keys (see the Go code below).

Consequently, I believe that this handling is a bug in the generated code which doesn't correctly differentiate those 2 cases even though it should.

## Swagger specification

``` yaml
info:
  title: Test API
  version: 0.1.0
schemes:
  - http
swagger: "2.0"

paths:
  /foo:
    post:
      consumes:
        - application/x-www-form-urlencoded
      parameters:
        - in: formData
          name: bar
          type: string
        - in: query
          name: baz
          type: string
      responses:
        200:
          description: dummy desc
```

## Handler code:

``` go
func handleFoo(p op.PostFooParams) middleware.Responder {
	fmt.Printf("%v: %q\t(%v)\n", p.Bar, swag.StringValue(p.Bar), p.HTTPRequest.PostForm)
	fmt.Printf("%v: %q\t(%v)\n", p.Baz, swag.StringValue(p.Baz), p.HTTPRequest.URL.Query())
	fmt.Println()

	return op.NewPostFooOK()
}
```

## Steps to reproduce

Use cURL to send empty query parameter and empty formData parameter.
``` sh
$ curl -X POST -H 'content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' localhost:<port>/foo?baz= -d bar=
``` 
Output of the code is then:
```
<nil>: ""       (map[bar:[]])
<nil>: ""       (map[baz:[]])
```

In case I don't specify those parameters at all:
``` sh
$ curl -X POST -H 'content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' localhost:9875/foo
```
I get the following output:
```
<nil>: ""       (map[])
<nil>: ""       (map[])
```

## Environment
swagger version: v0.28.0  
go version: 1.17.3
OS:  Linux/amd64 (Arch)
title: Empty value of query and formData parameters aliases to null

I am generating server code from `swagger.yaml`. The code makes intensive use of `uuid.UUID` (`github.com/google/uuid`), which is declared as `[16]byte`.

With @casualjim said:

body:
## Expected Behavior
Include an embedded struct in a response, label it with `swagger:allOf`, and expect to see a `$ref` in the generated schema.

## Actual Behavior
The fields are listed as properties

## Swagger specification

Note the `allOf` section for `userResponse`

This is the actual generated spec:

```yaml
responses:
  baseResponse:
    description: ""
    headers:
      error:
        description: An error message describing what went wrong
        type: string
      ok:
        description: Whether the request was successful or not
        type: boolean
  userResponse:
    description: ""
    schema:
      allOf:
      - properties:
          error:
            description: An error message describing what went wrong
            type: string
          ok:
            description: Whether the request was successful or not
            type: boolean
        type: object
      - properties:
          user:
            $ref: '#/definitions/User'
        type: object
```

And this is what should be generated:

```yaml
responses:
  baseResponse:
    description: ""
    headers:
      error:
        description: An error message describing what went wrong
        type: string
      ok:
        description: Whether the request was successful or not
        type: boolean
  userResponse:
    description: ""
    schema:
      allOf:
      - $ref: '#/responses/baseResponse'
      - properties:
          user:
            $ref: '#/definitions/User'
        type: object
```

## Steps to reproduce

```go
// swagger:response baseResponse
type BaseResponse struct {
	// An error message describing what went wrong
	Error string `json:"error,omitempty"`

	// Whether the request was successful or not
	OK bool `json:"ok"`
}

// swagger:response userResponse
type UserResponse struct {
	// in: body
	Body struct {
		// swagger:allOf
		BaseResponse

		User User `json:"user"`
	}
}
```

## Environment
swagger version: v0.29.0
go version: go1.18 linux/amd64
OS: Pop!_OS 21.04

When using go 1.18 the unit tests fail due to failing tests in codescan.

Digging into it a bit I found that it is because the order of the fields in the schemas built [here](https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/blob/42c184c09db16788c615be89f85deab6ae247c34/codescan/schema.go#L154) are different than they were in 1.17, and the tests rely on them being in a specific order.

operation "operation_name" has both formData and body parameters. Only one such In: type may be used for a given operation
```

I'm aware of this and I want the validator to ignore this error for the operation. Is there any markup/tag I can add to the specs to let the validator know to ignore the error? 

## Environment
swagger version: 0.25.0
go version: 1.14.2  
OS:  Linux
